猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but the main unresolved gap remains: A decisive molecule-specific discriminator between preserved emissive ICT and twisting-driven TICT relaxation is still missing. Best available assignment is ICT. Across the full run, ICT retains the strongest support from the molecule's donor-acceptor organization, extended conjugation, baseline S0/S1 context, and repeated verifier context that leans ICT overall. TICT remains credible because the molecule has substantial rotor capacity and the verifier explicitly preserved TICT-relevant discriminator themes, but no direct evidence in this run establishes a twisted emissive or relaxed TICT state for this specific molecule. The deciding gap that remains unresolved is the absence of a molecule-specific ICT-vs-TICT discriminator beyond mixed close-family precedent and low-yield artifact parsing.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is ICT (0.56) and top2 is TICT (0.29). I interpret the latest verifier evidence as high-information but mixed close-family context: it modestly supports ICT through donor-acceptor / solvatochromism / AIE-relevant precedent, while simultaneously preserving TICT through explicit multi-rotor, viscosity-dependence, dual-emission, and assignment-ambiguity themes. This verifier pass does not reduce any remaining portfolio coverage debt because coverage debt was already empty; instead it supports pairwise contraction by confirming that ICT versus TICT is the only still-relevant unresolved pair. No still-credible hypothesis remains untested, but TICT remains only indirectly weakened rather than directly excluded for this specific molecule. The case therefore remains legitimately in pairwise contraction, not portfolio screening. Closure justification is still collecting rather than sufficient, because verifier supplementation is already sufficient but the decisive molecule-specific discriminator is still missing. With round 17 of 20 and 4 rounds remaining including the current one, the case is not yet ready for decisive finalize; it is also not yet ready for best-available finalize because an additional bounded follow-up is still available and justified.

Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but the main unresolved gap remains: A decisive molecule-specific discriminator between preserved emissive ICT and twisting-driven TICT relaxation is still missing. Best available assignment is ICT. Across the full run, ICT retains the strongest support from the molecule's donor-acceptor organization, extended conjugation, baseline S0/S1 context, and repeated verifier context that leans ICT overall. TICT remains credible because the molecule has substantial rotor capacity and the verifier explicitly preserved TICT-relevant discriminator themes, but no direct evidence in this run establishes a twisted emissive or relaxed TICT state for this specific molecule. The deciding gap that remains unresolved is the absence of a molecule-specific ICT-vs-TICT discriminator beyond mixed close-family precedent and low-yield artifact parsing.

Verifier supplementation for ICT__vs__TICT is sufficient and high-information. It adds close-family context that modestly favors ICT overall while explicitly preserving TICT plausibility and clarifying the experimental discriminator themes that remain unresolved for this molecule.

Closure justification is still collecting rather than sufficient because the external supplement is already substantial but the run still lacks a molecule-specific ICT-versus-TICT discriminator. Because the remaining internal route is stalled and the round budget is now limited, the case is closed best-available with this unresolved gap stated explicitly.

Portfolio screening remains complete. The verifier evidence does not reduce additional portfolio coverage debt because no unscreened hypothesis remains; it only sharpens the already-screened ICT versus TICT pair.

Macro capabilities have already completed their portfolio-screening role. Verifier supplementation for ICT versus TICT is sufficient and high-information. The main limitation is the microscopic route: recent Amesp work has been parse-only, artifact-reuse-based, and has not produced a fresh molecule-specific discriminator under the available bounded capabilities.

Latest verifier supplement retrieved 4 close-family evidence cards covering ICT, TICT, dual emission, viscosity dependence, solvatochromism, AIE, multi-rotor behavior, restriction of intramolecular rotation, donor-acceptor systems, pyridinium, triarylamine, experimental discriminators, and assignment ambiguity. Planner interpretation: high information gain, mixed relation, modest net lean toward ICT, but no decisive molecule-specific closure. Recent internal evidence was another parse of the existing round_01 baseline bundle and added no new discriminative observable.
